examples.title: Energy Flow Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate energy at each trophic level based on 10% law
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateEnergyFlow
BEGIN
 INPUT: initialEnergy (from producers)
 SET energy[0] = initialEnergy
 
 FOR i = 1 TO 4 DO
 energy[i] = energy[i-1] * 0.10
 OUTPUT "Trophic Level", i, ":", energy[i], "Joules"
 END FOR
 
 OUTPUT "Total energy lost:", initialEnergy - energy[4]
END
examples.explanation: Demonstrates the 10% law of energy transfer. Starting from producer energy (e.g., 10,000 J), calculates energy available at each subsequent trophic level.

examples.title: Trophic Level Classification
examples.description: Algorithm to classify organisms into appropriate trophic levels
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ClassifyTrophicLevel
BEGIN
 INPUT: organismType
 
 SWITCH organismType DO
 CASE "plant":
 RETURN "Producer - Trophic Level 1"
 CASE "herbivore":
 RETURN "Primary Consumer - Trophic Level 2"
 CASE "carnivore":
 RETURN "Secondary Consumer - Trophic Level 3"
 CASE "top_predator":
 RETURN "Tertiary Consumer - Trophic Level 4"
 CASE "fungi":
 RETURN "Decomposer - Not a level (recycles)"
 DEFAULT:
 RETURN "Unknown classification"
 END SWITCH
END
examples.explanation: Simple classification system to assign organisms to their trophic levels based on their feeding habits.
